hero.title: Hi, I'm {name}.
hero.subtitle: {role} — {location}. I build end-to-end products with the modern web and the .NET ecosystem, and share what I learn on this blog.

about.heading: About
about.leadBefore: I see software development not just as a profession, but as a journey of
about.body: I'm a Computer Engineer building end-to-end solutions in the .NET ecosystem as well as with modern web technologies. I have hands-on experience with enterprise tools such as Entity Framework Core, Microsoft SQL Server, Azure DevOps and Microsoft SharePoint Management.

web.title: Web Development
web.description: Fast, SEO-friendly and responsive modern web applications with TypeScript, React, Next.js and Tailwind CSS.

desktop.title: Desktop Development
desktop.description: High-performance, reliable enterprise desktop software for Windows, built on WinForms and WPF.

services.title: Background Services
services.description: Long-running background jobs, Windows/Worker Service applications and scheduled data/queue processing systems.

api.title: API Design & Integrations
api.description: Secure, RESTful API design (.NET Web API) and data/service integration with a variety of third-party systems.

points: Building enterprise ERP and portal solutions with a .NET backend and modern frontend technologies.
Microservice architectures, integrations, and the optimization and management of web services.
Digitalizing business processes and driving performance improvement work.
Designing and coding internal operational applications, services and database structures.
API integrations and delivering on business units' reporting needs.
Managing IT infrastructure and developing/maintaining software for military information processing needs.
Building web-based projects, custom automation systems and e-commerce infrastructure from scratch.
Integrating frontend and backend components, and optimizing database queries.
Training in core algorithms, data structures and software engineering methodologies, plus graduation projects.

toolsPage.demoInTurkishNote: The interactive demo UI is currently in Turkish.

trkit.tagline: Zero-dependency utility kit for Turkish developers
trkit.description: Validation for Turkish ID numbers, tax numbers, IBANs, phone numbers and license plates; KVKK-friendly masking; Turkish İ/ı-aware text operations; number-to-words, VAT calculations and official holiday / business-day helpers verified against the Diyanet calendar. Pure functions — works everywhere, whether React, Vue, Angular or Node.
trkit.pageTitle: trkit — a Utility Kit for Turkish Developers
trkit.pageIntro: My open-source npm package: validation for Turkish ID numbers, tax numbers, IBANs, phone numbers and license plates; KVKK-friendly masking; Turkish İ/ı-aware text operations; number-to-words, VAT calculations and official holiday / business-day helpers verified against the Diyanet calendar. Pure functions — works everywhere, whether React, Vue, Angular or Node. The demos below run the package right in your browser.
trkit.metaDescription: trkit — try Türkiye-specific validation, formatting and KVKK masking functions live in your browser. A zero-dependency, open-source npm package.
trkit.disclaimer: Sample values are synthetic; they are not real personal data. trkit only performs format/checksum validation — it never queries any government service, does not process personal data, and is not a KVKK/GDPR compliance guarantee. Nothing you enter leaves your browser.

locakit.tagline: Deterministic i18n engine for AI agents
locakit.description: Keeps your locale JSON files in sync with your agent in multilingual projects: it detects missing and stale keys with a lockfile, your existing AI agent (Claude Code and friends) does the translating with full codebase context, and locakit validates placeholder parity, glossary terms and Turkish language-pack rules before writing safely to JSON. No translation API, no SaaS account, no token bill.
locakit.pageTitle: locakit — a Deterministic i18n Engine for AI Agents
locakit.pageIntroBefore: Every translation tool ships its own LLM pipeline: API keys, SaaS accounts, token bills. Meanwhile, the agent you use all day already reads your codebase.
locakit.pageIntroEmphasis: locakit splits the job at the right seam:
locakit.pageIntroAfter: your agent does the translating; locakit handles the deterministic work — detecting missing/stale keys, validating placeholders and glossary terms, and writing JSON safely.
locakit.metaDescription: locakit — a deterministic i18n engine for AI agents. It detects missing and stale translation keys, your agent translates with context, and locakit validates and writes safely. Try the validation engine live in your browser.

diff.detail: Detects missing and stale keys via the lockfile and returns them as JSON, together with your context and glossary.

translate.detail: Claude Code reads where each key is used in the code — if "Home" is a nav link it becomes the navigation label, not the building. Knowing the project's tone keeps the register consistent.

apply.detail: Writes the patched translations while preserving the source key order; keys that don't exist in the source are rejected (hallucination safety).

check.detail: Validates placeholder parity, glossary terms and language-pack rules; on errors the CI build fails.

locakit.liveIntroBefore: The example below is intentionally broken: it contains a missing key, a placeholder mismatch, an orphan key and two classic mistakes caught by the Turkish language pack. Press the

utilkit.tagline: A zero-dependency devkit for everyday needs
utilkit.description: Unicode-safe Base64 encoding, correct string length (visual character + UTF-8 byte), and safe JSON parsing. Pure functions — works everywhere, whether React, Vue, Angular or Node.
utilkit.pageIntro: My open-source npm package: Unicode-safe Base64 encoding, correct string length (visual character + UTF-8 byte), and safe JSON parsing. Pure functions — works everywhere, whether React, Vue, Angular or Node. The demos below run the package right in your browser.
utilkit.metaDescription: utilkit — try Unicode-safe Base64 encoding, correct string length and safe JSON parsing functions live in your browser. A zero-dependency, open-source npm package.
